key_concepts: Matter, its classification, and properties (physical/chemical)
Laws of chemical combination: conservation of mass, definite proportions, multiple proportions
Mole concept: 1 mole = 6.022 x 10^23 particles (Avogadro's number)
Molarity, molality, mole fraction, and percentage composition
Empirical vs molecular formula; limiting reagent in reactions
key_formulae: Molarity (M) = moles of solute / volume of solution (L)
Molality (m) = moles of solute / mass of solvent (kg)
short_tricks: Remember 'MMM' order: Molarity depends on volume, Molality on mass - Molality is temperature independent
Empirical formula = simplest whole-number ratio; molecular = (Empirical)n

key_concepts: Discovery of electron, proton, neutron; Rutherford & Bohr models
Quantum numbers (n, l, m, s) and their significance
Aufbau principle, Pauli exclusion principle, Hund's rule
de Broglie equation and Heisenberg uncertainty principle
Electronic configuration of elements
short_tricks: 'n-l rule (Aufbau)': lower (n+l) fills first; if equal, lower n fills first
Remember order: 1s 2s 2p 3s 3p 4s 3d 4p 5s 4d 5p 6s 4f 5d 6p 7s

chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
key_concepts: Modern periodic law and long form of periodic table
s, p, d, f block classification
Periodic trends: atomic radius, ionization enthalpy, electron gain enthalpy, electronegativity
Anomalous properties of the first element in each group
key_formulae: Atomic radius decreases across a period, increases down a group
short_tricks: 'Zig-zag rule' for ionization enthalpy exceptions at Be/B and N/O
FONClBrISCH mnemonic for electronegativity order (high to low)

key_concepts: Ionic, covalent, and coordinate bonding; Lewis structures
VSEPR theory for molecular geometry
Valence bond theory and hybridization (sp, sp2, sp3, sp3d, sp3d2)
Molecular orbital theory (MOT) for diatomic molecules
Hydrogen bonding and its types (intramolecular/intermolecular)
short_tricks: 'AXN method' to quickly assign VSEPR geometry from Lewis structure
Remember: more s-character = more bond angle (sp > sp2 > sp3)

key_concepts: System, surroundings; types of systems (open/closed/isolated)
First law of thermodynamics: ΔU = q + w
Enthalpy, Hess's Law, enthalpies of formation/combustion/reaction
Second law, entropy, Gibbs free energy: ΔG = ΔH - TΔS
Spontaneity criteria using ΔG
key_formulae: ΔG < 0: spontaneous; ΔG = 0: equilibrium; ΔG > 0: non-spontaneous
short_tricks: Hess's Law shortcut: total enthalpy change is path-independent - just add/subtract given reactions

key_concepts: Physical vs chemical equilibrium; dynamic nature of equilibrium
Law of mass action, equilibrium constant Kc and Kp
Le Chatelier's principle and factors affecting equilibrium
Acids, bases (Arrhenius, Bronsted-Lowry, Lewis), pH scale
Buffer solutions, common ion effect, solubility product (Ksp)
short_tricks: 'Stress relief' mnemonic for Le Chatelier: system shifts to relieve applied stress (conc/temp/pressure)

key_concepts: Oxidation and reduction in terms of electron transfer
Oxidation number rules and balancing redox equations
Oxidizing and reducing agents
Ion-electron (half-reaction) method and oxidation number method
key_formulae: Oxidation number sum in a neutral compound = 0; in an ion = charge on ion
short_tricks: 'OIL RIG': Oxidation Is Loss, Reduction Is Gain (of electrons)

key_concepts: Position of hydrogen in the periodic table; isotopes (protium, deuterium, tritium)
Preparation, properties and uses of hydrogen
Hydrides: ionic, covalent, and interstitial
Water: structure, hydrogen bonding, hardness of water
Hydrogen peroxide: structure, preparation, and uses
short_tricks: Hydrogen behaves like both alkali metals (group 1) and halogens (group 17) - remember 'dual behaviour'

key_concepts: Group 1 (alkali metals) and Group 2 (alkaline earth metals) general trends
Anomalous behaviour of lithium and beryllium (diagonal relationship)
Important compounds: NaOH, Na2CO3, CaO, CaCO3, Plaster of Paris
Biological importance of Na, K, Mg, Ca
short_tricks: Diagonal relationship: Li~Mg, Be~Al - similar properties due to similar charge/size ratio

key_concepts: General trends in group 13 (boron family) and group 14 (carbon family)
Boron: structure, borax, boric acid, diborane
Carbon: allotropes (diamond, graphite, fullerenes), catenation
Silicon: silicates, zeolites, silicones
short_tricks: Inert pair effect: heavier p-block elements favor lower oxidation state (e.g., Pb2+ more stable than Pb4+)

chapter: Organic Chemistry - Some Basic Principles and Techniques
key_concepts: IUPAC nomenclature of organic compounds
Structural isomerism (chain, position, functional, metamerism)
Electronic effects: inductive, resonance, hyperconjugation, electromeric
Reaction intermediates: carbocation, carbanion, free radical
Purification techniques: distillation, chromatography, crystallization
short_tricks: 'I Have Really Enough' mnemonic for effects: Inductive, Hyperconjugation, Resonance, Electromeric - all increase stability

key_concepts: Alkanes: preparation, conformations, free radical substitution
Alkenes: preparation, Markovnikov's & anti-Markovnikov's (peroxide) rule
Alkynes: acidic character, addition reactions
Aromatic hydrocarbons: benzene structure, aromaticity (Huckel's rule 4n+2), electrophilic substitution
key_formulae: Huckel's rule: aromatic if planar, cyclic, conjugated, with (4n+2) π electrons
short_tricks: Markovnikov: 'rich gets richer' - H adds to carbon with more H already attached

key_concepts: Atmospheric pollution: tropospheric and stratospheric
Greenhouse effect and global warming
Ozone layer depletion and its causes (CFCs)
Water and soil pollution; green chemistry principles
short_tricks: Remember CFC = ChloroFluoroCarbon = major ozone depleter

key_concepts: Classification of solids: crystalline vs amorphous
Unit cells: primitive, body-centred, face-centred
Packing efficiency and calculations for SC, BCC, FCC
Point defects: Schottky and Frenkel defects
Electrical and magnetic properties of solids
short_tricks: Schottky = missing ion pairs (density decreases); Frenkel = ion displaced (density unchanged)

key_concepts: Types of solutions and concentration terms
Raoult's Law and ideal/non-ideal solutions
Colligative properties: relative lowering of vapor pressure, elevation in boiling point, depression in freezing point, osmotic pressure
Van't Hoff factor for electrolyte dissociation/association
short_tricks: Van't Hoff factor i > 1 for dissociation, i < 1 for association

key_concepts: Electrolytic and galvanic cells; electrode potential
Nernst equation and its applications
Conductance, molar conductivity, Kohlrausch's law
Batteries (primary/secondary) and fuel cells
Corrosion as an electrochemical process
key_formulae: Nernst equation: E = E° - (0.0591/n) log Q (at 298K)
short_tricks: Anode = Oxidation (both start with vowels); Cathode = Reduction (both start with consonants)

key_concepts: Rate of reaction and factors affecting it
Order and molecularity of reactions
Integrated rate laws for zero and first order reactions
Arrhenius equation and activation energy
Collision theory basics
short_tricks: Order is experimental (from rate law); molecularity is theoretical (from mechanism, always whole number)

key_concepts: Adsorption: physisorption vs chemisorption
Catalysis: homogeneous, heterogeneous, and enzyme catalysis
Colloids: types, properties (Tyndall effect, Brownian motion)
Emulsions and their types
short_tricks: Physisorption: weak van der Waals, reversible, multilayer; Chemisorption: strong chemical bond, irreversible, monolayer

chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements
key_concepts: Occurrence of metals: minerals and ores
Concentration of ore, extraction methods (pyro/hydro/electrometallurgy)
Thermodynamic principles (Ellingham diagram)
Refining methods: electrolytic refining, zone refining, vapor phase refining
short_tricks: Ellingham diagram: more negative ΔG line reduces the oxide of the less negative one

key_concepts: Group 15: nitrogen family - NH3, HNO3 preparation and properties
Group 16: oxygen family - O3, H2SO4 (contact process)
Group 17: halogens - properties, interhalogen compounds
Group 18: noble gases - occurrence, compounds of xenon
short_tricks: Contact process for H2SO4 uses V2O5 catalyst; Ostwald process for HNO3 uses Pt-Rh catalyst

chapter: The d and f Block Elements
key_concepts: General properties of transition elements: variable oxidation states, colored ions, catalytic property
Interstitial compounds and alloy formation
Lanthanoid contraction and its consequences
Actinoids: general characteristics
short_tricks: Lanthanoid contraction causes 4d and 5d elements of same group to have near-identical atomic radii (e.g. Zr~Hf)

key_concepts: Werner's theory; ligands, coordination number, IUPAC nomenclature
Isomerism in coordination compounds (structural & stereo)
Valence bond theory and crystal field theory (CFT)
Crystal field splitting in octahedral and tetrahedral complexes
Applications: biological systems, extraction of metals, qualitative analysis
key_formulae: CFSE (octahedral): high spin/low spin depends on Δo vs pairing energy
short_tricks: 'EAN rule' (Effective Atomic Number) often equals the atomic number of the next noble gas for stable complexes

key_concepts: Nomenclature and preparation of alkyl/aryl halides
Nucleophilic substitution: SN1 vs SN2 mechanisms
Elimination reactions and Zaitsev's rule
Uses and environmental effects (DDT, freons)
short_tricks: SN1: 3° > 2° > 1° (carbocation stability); SN2: 1° > 2° > 3° (steric hindrance)

key_concepts: Nomenclature, preparation, and physical properties
Distinguishing reactions: Lucas test, ferric chloride test for phenols
Acidity of phenols vs alcohols; electrophilic substitution in phenols
Williamson ether synthesis
short_tricks: Phenol is more acidic than alcohol due to resonance stabilization of the phenoxide ion

key_concepts: Nomenclature and structure of carbonyl compounds
Nucleophilic addition reactions; aldol condensation, Cannizzaro reaction
Distinguishing tests: Tollens', Fehling's, iodoform test
Acidity of carboxylic acids and effect of substituents
short_tricks: Tollens' test (silver mirror) = aldehydes only; Iodoform test = CH3CO- group or CH3CH(OH)- group

key_concepts: Classification, nomenclature, and preparation of amines
Basicity of amines and comparison (aliphatic vs aromatic)
Distinguishing 1°, 2°, 3° amines (Hinsberg's test)
Diazonium salts and their synthetic importance
short_tricks: Aliphatic amine basicity in gas phase: 3° > 2° > 1°; in aqueous solution often 2° > 1° > 3° due to solvation/steric effects

key_concepts: Carbohydrates: classification, structure of glucose/fructose, mutarotation
Proteins: amino acids, peptide bond, structure levels (primary to quaternary)
Enzymes and vitamins classification
Nucleic acids: DNA and RNA structure, base pairing
short_tricks: DNA base pairing: A-T (2 H-bonds), G-C (3 H-bonds) - 'G-C bonds are Great & Cozy (stronger)'

key_concepts: Classification: natural vs synthetic, addition vs condensation polymers
Important polymers: polythene, nylon-6,6, terylene, buna-N/S rubber
Biodegradable polymers (PHBV, nylon-2-nylon-6)
Rubber vulcanization
short_tricks: Addition polymers form from monomers with multiple bonds (e.g. alkenes); condensation polymers release small molecules like H2O

key_concepts: Drugs and their classification (analgesics, antibiotics, antiseptics)
Mechanism of drug action, enzyme inhibition
Chemicals in food: preservatives, artificial sweeteners
Cleansing agents: soaps and synthetic detergents
short_tricks: Antibiotics: bactericidal (kill bacteria, e.g. penicillin) vs bacteriostatic (inhibit growth, e.g. erythromycin)
